cards.back: A type of parallel computing architecture where multiple computers are connected and share resources, each with its own memory space
The process of managing and synchronizing multiple processes or threads in parallel computing, to ensure they work together efficiently and effectively
A type of parallel computing architecture where a single shared memory space is accessed by multiple processors or threads, to share data and reduce communication overhead

A type of parallel computing architecture where each process or thread has its own private memory space, and communication between them is done through message passing or other synchronization mechanisms
In distributed-memory systems, coordination is achieved through message passing, where processes communicate with each other to share data, coordinate tasks, and synchronize their execution
